description: Cross-language conformance for Config Bus `mcp.middleware` loading. Each case supplies a raw JSON array that a bridge would receive from `Config.get("mcp.middleware")`; the bridge's middleware_builder must produce the documented middleware names and accept the documented config.

test_cases.description: An empty list yields zero middleware instances.
A bare `retry` entry builds a RetryMiddleware with default config.
`retry` with custom keys is accepted and forwarded to RetryConfig.
A bare `logging` entry builds a LoggingMiddleware with log_inputs/outputs/errors all true.
`error_history` accepts max_entries_per_module and max_total_entries shorthand keys.
Ordering of the input list is preserved in the output list.

error_cases.description: Unknown `type` value raises a config error.
Entry without a `type` key raises a config error.
A non-object entry (e.g. a string) raises a config error.
Keys other than max_entries_per_module / max_total_entries are rejected.

known_divergences: apcore-rust's ErrorHistory has a lossy shorthand: with only max_entries_per_module set, max_total_entries defaults to max_entries_per_module * 100. Python and TypeScript derive their own defaults (50 and 1000). Conformance cases only assert middleware names, not internal limit values.
apcore-rust's RetryMiddleware and ErrorHistoryMiddleware report different debug names than Python/TypeScript; all three SDKs agree on the public `name()` string which is asserted here.
